action.reasoning: Observation: After 11 iterations the best nav_seek_success is still 0.0. The GPS-only proportional controller reached only ~82 safe_follow_steps in 50-episode eval (found_human_rate 0.15). The parameter sweep showed the local controller can maintain ~214 safe steps once the human is found, so the bottleneck is long-range navigation and approach time. Analysis: Earlier custom pathfinder attempts failed because extracting robot yaw from localization_sensor/base_rot gave wrong coordinate frames. The delta-angle trick computes the local bearing to any waypoint without knowing robot yaw: local_angle_to_waypoint = gps_angle_to_human + (global_yaw_to_waypoint - global_yaw_to_human). Combining navmesh pathfinding with this trick yields reliable long-range approach. Once close (<3.0 m), we fall back to the sweep-tuned proportional local controller with angle gating and safety clamping. Decision: Rewrite model.py with (1) navmesh ShortestPath for long-range lookahead waypoints, (2) delta-angle steering, (3) stateful episode reset, and (4) robust local controller. Update predict.py to pass env and reset state. Run a 2-episode local sanity test, then submit the 50-episode eval.
